misc.c(3) Library Functions Manual misc.c(3)

NAME

misc.c - Miscellaneous stubs for WASM.

SYNOPSIS

Typedefs


typedef struct video_fps_counter_t video_fps_counter_t
typedef void render_file_t
typedef void media_source_t
typedef void symbol_cache_t
typedef void(* signal_handler_t) (int)
typedef void mdns_query_t
typedef void mdns_result_t

Functions

Stop full-duplex audio. void audio_terminate_portaudio_final (void)
Terminate PortAudio and free all device resources. void debug_sync_cleanup_thread (void)
Stop and clean up background debug thread. void fps_init (video_fps_counter_t *fps, double target_fps)
uint64_t fps_frame_ns (video_fps_counter_t *fps)
tty_info_t get_current_tty (void)
Get current TTY information. terminal_capabilities_t apply_color_mode_override (terminal_capabilities_t caps)
Apply command-line overrides to detected capabilities. render_file_t * render_file_create (const char *output_file)
void render_file_set_audio_source (render_file_t *rf, media_source_t *source)
void render_file_set_video_source (render_file_t *rf, media_source_t *source)
void render_file_write_frame (render_file_t *rf, const uint8_t *data, size_t len)
void render_file_destroy (render_file_t *rf)
void render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration (render_file_t *rf, uint64_t duration_ns)
asciichat_error_t terminal_clear_scrollback (int fd)
Clear terminal scrollback buffer. asciichat_error_t terminal_reset (int fd)
Reset terminal to default state. void symbol_cache_destroy (symbol_cache_t *cache)
void terminal_stop_resize_detection (void)
No-op stub on POSIX platforms (no resize detection thread). void known_hosts_destroy (void)
Cleanup function to free cached known_hosts path. void platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache (void)
Cleanup the binary PATH cache. asciichat_error_t platform_restore_timer_resolution (void)
Restore default timer precision. h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (int width, int height, double fps)
signal_handler_t platform_signal (int sig, signal_handler_t handler)
Set a signal handler. void platform_force_exit (int exit_code)
Forcefully terminate the process immediately without cleanup. void set_interrupt_callback (void(*callback)(void))
void signal_exit (void)
bool should_exit (void)
mdns_query_t * ui_mdns_query (const char *service)
mdns_result_t * ui_mdns_get_best_address (mdns_query_t *query)
mdns_result_t * ui_mdns_select (mdns_result_t *results)
void ui_mdns_free_results (mdns_result_t *results)

Detailed Description

Miscellaneous stubs for WASM.

Function Documentation

terminal_capabilities_t apply_color_mode_override (terminal_capabilities_t caps)

Apply command-line overrides to detected capabilities.

Parameters

caps Terminal capabilities structure to modify

Returns

Modified terminal capabilities structure

Applies any command-line option overrides to the detected terminal capabilities. Overrides may include:

  • Force color mode (--color, --no-color, --256, --truecolor)
  • Force UTF-8 mode (--utf8)
  • Render mode selection (--bg, --fg, --half-block)
  • Palette selection (--palette)

Note

Returns a modified copy of the input structure.

Overrides take precedence over detected capabilities.

46   return caps;
47 }

tty_info_t get_current_tty (void )


Get current TTY information.


Returns


TTY information structure with file descriptor and
  path


Retrieves information about the current TTY (terminal). Returns
    file descriptor for TTY access, device path, and ownership information.
    Useful for advanced terminal operations that require direct TTY access.




Note


File descriptor may need to be closed if owns_fd is true.



TTY path is platform-specific (Unix: /dev/tty, Windows: CON,
    etc.).




Definition at line 40 of file misc.c.

41   tty_info_t tty = {0};
42   return tty;
43 }

void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 137 of file misc.c.


137                                                     {
138   (void)callback;
139 }






Referenced by client_main(), and
  discovery_main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 144 of file misc.c.


144                        {
145   return false;
146 }






Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_attempt_websocket(),
    display_full_reset(), main(),
    protocol_start_connection(), server_connection_establish(),
    session_client_like_run(), session_pipeline_run_main(), and
    session_render_loop().






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 141 of file misc.c.


141                        {
142 }






Referenced by client_main(), main(), and
    session_render_loop().
